task_completion.description: Navigate to the coordinates of Wallace Creek (35.2715°N, 119.8272°W)
task_completion.visual_evidence: The map viewport should show the Carrizo Plain area, specifically the Wallace Creek feature (a distinct dog-leg channel). Coordinates in the status bar or search box should match approximately 35.2715, -119.8272.

task_completion.description: Open the ruler or measurement tool in the application
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Ruler' or 'Measure' dialog box should be open and visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Draw a measurement line across the fault offset
task_completion.visual_evidence: A measurement line (usually yellow or colored) should be visible on the map, spanning the distance between the upstream and downstream segments of the offset channel along the fault trace.

task_completion.description: Create a placemark named 'Wallace Creek Offset'
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'New Placemark' or 'Properties' dialog should be open with the Name field set to 'Wallace Creek Offset'.

task_completion.description: Enter the measured offset value (approx 128m) in the description
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Description field of the placemark dialog should contain a numeric value in meters. Based on verified data, this value should be approximately 128m (accept 120m-135m).

task_completion.description: Save the placemark to the specified path
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save File' or 'Save Place As' dialog should be visible, showing the filename 'wallace_creek_offset.kml' and the path '/home/ga/Documents/'.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent uses a GIS application (e.g., Google Earth Pro) interface
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show the interface of a mapping application (menus, map view, toolbars), not just a code editor or terminal.

integrity.description: Verify the reported value matches the tool's measurement
integrity.visual_evidence: If the Ruler tool result is visible simultaneously with the Placemark description, the values should be consistent (e.g., the ruler shows ~128m and the description says 128m).

integrity.description: Ensure the file was not generated purely via command line
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent should not be seen simply running a script or command (like `echo ... > file.kml`) to create the output file without interacting with the map software.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target location: 35.2715, -119.8272. The geological feature is a stream channel offset by the San Andreas Fault. Verified scientific measurement of the offset is approximately 128 meters. For manual measurements in a tool like Google Earth, accept values between 120m and 135m.
